      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

query_hardware_features("GeForce RTX 3060 Ti", "all")

{
  "found": true,

      "feature_id": "tensor_cores",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom cut_cross_entropy import linear_cross_entropy\n\nB, T, D, V = 2, 16, 1024, 32000\nembeddings = torch.randn(B, T, D, device='cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16, requires_grad=True)\nclassifier = torch.randn(V, D, device='cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16, requires_grad=True)\nlabels = torch.randint(0, V, (B, T), device='cuda')\nloss = linear_cross_entropy(embeddings, classifier, labels, shift=1)\nloss.backward()",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

Removes CPU-decode bottleneck for CV/imaging. ~2.18x faster JPEG loading. No-op for text/LLM training.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom nvidia import nvimgcodec\n\ndecoder = nvimgcodec.Decoder()\nnv_img = decoder.read('sample.jp2')\ncap = nv_img.to_dlpack()\ntorch_img = torch.from_dlpack(cap)\nassert torch_img.is_cuda\nbatch = torch_img.permute(2, 0, 1).unsqueeze(0).contiguous()",

      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom nvidia import nvimgcodec\n\ndecoder = nvimgcodec.Decoder()\nnv_img = decoder.read('sample.jp2')\ncap = nv_img.to_dlpack()\ntorch_img = torch.from_dlpack(cap)\nassert torch_img.is_cuda\nbatch = torch_img.permute(2, 0, 1).unsqueeze(0).contiguous()",

      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",
      "source_urls": [
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.13226"
      ],
      "support_level": "supported",
      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,

      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom soap import SOAP\n\nmodel = nn.Linear(16, 4)\nx = torch.randn(8, 16)\nloss = model(x).pow(2).mean()\noptim = SOAP(model.parameters(), lr=3e-3, betas=(0.95, 0.95), weight_decay=0.01, precondition_frequency=10)\noptim.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim.step()",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.13226"

      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom torchao.float8 import Float8LinearConfig, convert_to_float8_training\nfrom torchao.float8 import precompute_float8_dynamic_scale_for_fsdp\nfrom torch.distributed.fsdp import fully_shard\n\nmodel = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(4096, 4096), nn.GELU(), nn.Linear(4096, 4096)).bfloat16().cuda()\nconfig = Float8LinearConfig(enable_fsdp_float8_all_gather=True)\nconvert_to_float8_training(model, config=config)\nfor layer in model:\n    if isinstance(layer, nn.Linear):\n        fully_shard(layer)\nfully_shard(model)\n# In the distributed training loop, call after optimizer.step():\nprecompute_float8_dynamic_scale_for_fsdp(model)",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.13226"

      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom torchao.float8 import Float8LinearConfig, convert_to_float8_training\nfrom torchao.float8 import precompute_float8_dynamic_scale_for_fsdp\nfrom torch.distributed.fsdp import fully_shard\n\nmodel = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(4096, 4096), nn.GELU(), nn.Linear(4096, 4096)).bfloat16().cuda()\nconfig = Float8LinearConfig(enable_fsdp_float8_all_gather=True)\nconvert_to_float8_training(model, config=config)\nfor layer in model:\n    if isinstance(layer, nn.Linear):\n        fully_shard(layer)\nfully_shard(model)\n# In the distributed training loop, call after optimizer.step():\nprecompute_float8_dynamic_scale_for_fsdp(model)",

      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

query_hardware_features("GeForce RTX 5060 Ti 8GB", "all")

{
  "found": true,
  "node_id": "hw:nvidia.blackwell.geforce_rtx_5060_ti_8gb.spec",
  "gpu_name": "GeForce RTX 5060 Ti 8GB",
  "stage_filter": null,
  "feature_count": 21,
  "features": [
    {
      "feature_id": "tensor_cores",
      "category": "tensor_core",
      "description": "Set torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark=True so that when a cuDNN\nconvolution is called with a new set of size parameters, cuDNN runs\nmultiple algorithms, benchmarks them to find the fastest one, and\nreuses it for the rest of the process for the same size parameters.\nDisabling it (benchmark=False) makes cuDNN deterministically select\nan algorithm, possibly at the cost of reduced performance. The\nseparate torch.backends.cudnn.deterministic flag controls\nreproducibility and is distinct from the benchmark setting; combine\nit with seeded RNGs (Python, NumPy, PyTorch) when you need\nreproducible runs.",

      "description": "Muon optimizer: for 2D weight matrices, replaces the momentum-smoothed gradient with its nearest semi-orthogonal matrix via a fixed 5-step Newton-Schulz iteration (no SVD); 1D params stay on AdamW. Matches AdamW final loss using ~52% of training FLOPs.",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\nmuon_params = []\nadamw_params = []\nfor name, p in model.named_parameters():\n    if not p.requires_grad:\n        continue\n    is_hidden_2d = p.ndim == 2 and 'embed' not in name and 'lm_head' not in name\n    if is_hidden_2d:\n        muon_params.append(p)\n    else:\n        adamw_params.append(p)\n\noptim_muon = torch.optim.Muon(muon_params, lr=0.02, weight_decay=0.1, momentum=0.95, nesterov=True, ns_steps=5, adjust_lr_fn='match_rms_adamw')\noptim_adamw = torch.optim.AdamW(adamw_params, lr=3e-4, weight_decay=0.01)\n\noptim_muon.zero_grad()\noptim_adamw.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim_muon.step()\noptim_adamw.step()",

      "feature_id": "cut_cross_entropy",
      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Cut Cross-Entropy: fuses a linear classifier/logit projection with the cross-entropy loss so the (N x V) logits tensor is never materialized in global memory [https://github.com/apple/ml-cross-entropy]; chunked online log-sum-exp in SRAM.       "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Gram Newton-Schulz symmetric/triangular fused GEMM: the Newton-Schulz step (used by Muon) is a symmetric product; a triangular fused kernel removes the wasted half of multiplies plus a DRAM round-trip cuBLAS incurs. Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "gram_newton_schulz_symmetric_gemm",
      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Gram Newton-Schulz symmetric/triangular fused GEMM: the Newton-Schulz step (used by Muon) is a symmetric product; a triangular fused kernel removes the wasted half of multiplies plus a DRAM round-trip cuBLAS incurs. Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",
      "api_symbols": [
        "custom_kernel:gram_newton_schulz",

      "recommendation_scope": "muon_newton_schulz_kernel_candidate",
      "limitations": "custom Gram/Newton-Schulz kernel optimization; recommended=true means candidate only when Muon/Newton-Schulz orthogonalization is material in the step profile, not a generic GEMM replacement; intended for Hopper/Blackwell-class tensor-core kernels; source is primary technical blog, not official vendor doc; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true",
      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: suggest this only after Muon has been selected and profiler evidence shows orthogonalization dominates; otherwise prefer normal optimizer tuning."
    }
  ]
}

      "description": "Muon optimizer: for 2D weight matrices, replaces the momentum-smoothed gradient with its nearest semi-orthogonal matrix via a fixed 5-step Newton-Schulz iteration (no SVD); 1D params stay on AdamW. Matches AdamW final loss using ~52% of training FLOPs.",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\nmuon_params = []\nadamw_params = []\nfor name, p in model.named_parameters():\n    if not p.requires_grad:\n        continue\n    is_hidden_2d = p.ndim == 2 and 'embed' not in name and 'lm_head' not in name\n    if is_hidden_2d:\n        muon_params.append(p)\n    else:\n        adamw_params.append(p)\n\noptim_muon = torch.optim.Muon(muon_params, lr=0.02, weight_decay=0.1, momentum=0.95, nesterov=True, ns_steps=5, adjust_lr_fn='match_rms_adamw')\noptim_adamw = torch.optim.AdamW(adamw_params, lr=3e-4, weight_decay=0.01)\n\noptim_muon.zero_grad()\noptim_adamw.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim_muon.step()\noptim_adamw.step()",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Gram Newton-Schulz symmetric/triangular fused GEMM: the Newton-Schulz step (used by Muon) is a symmetric product; a triangular fused kernel removes the wasted half of multiplies plus a DRAM round-trip cuBLAS incurs. Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

query_hardware_features("NVIDIA A10", "all")

{
  "found": true,
  "node_id": "hw:nvidia.ampere.a10.spec",
  "gpu_name": "NVIDIA A10",
  "stage_filter": null,
  "feature_count": 15,
  "features": [
    {
      "feature_id": "tensor_cores",
      "category": "tensor_core",
      "description": "Set torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark=True so that when a cuDNN\nconvolution is called with a new set of size parameters, cuDNN runs\nmultiple algorithms, benchmarks them to find the fastest one, and\nreuses it for the rest of the process for the same size parameters.\nDisabling it (benchmark=False) makes cuDNN deterministically select\nan algorithm, possibly at the cost of reduced performance.       "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

query_hardware_features("NVIDIA A100 PCIe 40GB", "all")

{
  "found": true,
  "node_id": "hw:nvidia.ampere.a100_pcie_40gb.spec",
  "gpu_name": "NVIDIA A100 PCIe 40GB",
  "stage_filter": null,
  "feature_count": 16,
  "features": [
    {
      "feature_id": "tensor_cores",
      "category": "tensor_core",
      "description": "Set torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark=True so that when a cuDNN\nconvolution is called with a new set of size parameters, cuDNN runs\nmultiple algorithms, benchmarks them to find the fastest one, and\nreuses it for the rest of the process for the same size parameters.\nDisabling it (benchmark=False) makes cuDNN deterministically select\nan algorithm, possibly at the cost of reduced performance.       "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

query_hardware_features("NVIDIA A100 PCIe 80GB", "all")

{
  "found": true,
  "node_id": "hw:nvidia.ampere.a100_pcie_80gb.spec",
  "gpu_name": "NVIDIA A100 PCIe 80GB",
  "stage_filter": null,
  "feature_count": 16,
  "features": [
    {
      "feature_id": "tensor_cores",
      "category": "tensor_core",
      "description": "Set torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark=True so that when a cuDNN\nconvolution is called with a new set of size parameters, cuDNN runs\nmultiple algorithms, benchmarks them to find the fastest one, and\nreuses it for the rest of the process for the same size parameters.\nDisabling it (benchmark=False) makes cuDNN deterministically select\nan algorithm, possibly at the cost of reduced performance.       "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

query_hardware_features("NVIDIA A100 SXM4 40GB", "all")

{
  "found": true,
  "node_id": "hw:nvidia.ampere.a100_sxm4_40gb.spec",
  "gpu_name": "NVIDIA A100 SXM4 40GB",
  "stage_filter": null,
  "feature_count": 16,
  "features": [
    {
      "feature_id": "tensor_cores",
      "category": "tensor_core",
      "description": "Set torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark=True so that when a cuDNN\nconvolution is called with a new set of size parameters, cuDNN runs\nmultiple algorithms, benchmarks them to find the fastest one, and\nreuses it for the rest of the process for the same size parameters.\nDisabling it (benchmark=False) makes cuDNN deterministically select\nan algorithm, possibly at the cost of reduced performance.       "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

query_hardware_features("NVIDIA A100 SXM4 80GB", "all")

{
  "found": true,
  "node_id": "hw:nvidia.ampere.a100_sxm4_80gb.spec",
  "gpu_name": "NVIDIA A100 SXM4 80GB",
  "stage_filter": null,
  "feature_count": 16,
  "features": [
    {
      "feature_id": "tensor_cores",
      "category": "tensor_core",
      "description": "Set torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark=True so that when a cuDNN\nconvolution is called with a new set of size parameters, cuDNN runs\nmultiple algorithms, benchmarks them to find the fastest one, and\nreuses it for the rest of the process for the same size parameters.\nDisabling it (benchmark=False) makes cuDNN deterministically select\nan algorithm, possibly at the cost of reduced performance.       "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

query_hardware_features("NVIDIA A16", "all")

{
  "found": true,
  "node_id": "hw:nvidia.ampere.a16.spec",
  "gpu_name": "NVIDIA A16",
  "stage_filter": null,
  "feature_count": 14,
  "features": [
    {
      "feature_id": "tensor_cores",
      "category": "tensor_core",
      "description": "Set torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark=True so that when a cuDNN\nconvolution is called with a new set of size parameters, cuDNN runs\nmultiple algorithms, benchmarks them to find the fastest one, and\nreuses it for the rest of the process for the same size parameters.\nDisabling it (benchmark=False) makes cuDNN deterministically select\nan algorithm, possibly at the cost of reduced performance.       "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Gram Newton-Schulz symmetric/triangular fused GEMM: the Newton-Schulz step (used by Muon) is a symmetric product; a triangular fused kernel removes the wasted half of multiplies plus a DRAM round-trip cuBLAS incurs. Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

      "example_code": "import os\nimport torch\nimport torch.distributed as dist\nimport torch.distributed._symmetric_memory as symm_mem\n\nrank = int(os.environ.get('LOCAL_RANK', '0'))\ndevice = torch.device('cuda', rank)\nopts = dist.ProcessGroupNCCL.Options()\nopts.config.cta_policy = dist.ProcessGroupNCCL.NCCL_CTA_POLICY_ZERO\ndist.init_process_group(backend='nccl', pg_options=opts, device_id=device)\nworld_size = dist.get_world_size()\n\nsymm_mem.set_backend('NCCL')\ngroup_name = dist.group.WORLD.group_name\ninp = symm_mem.empty(1024 * 1024, device=device)\nout = symm_mem.empty(1024 * 1024 * world_size, device=device)\nsymm_mem.rendezvous(inp, group=group_name)\nsymm_mem.rendezvous(out, group=group_name)\nwork = dist.all_gather_into_tensor(out, inp, async_op=True)\n# compute can be scheduled here on independent work\nwork.wait()",

      "feature_id": "muon_optimizer",
      "category": "optimizer",
      "description": "Muon optimizer: for 2D weight matrices, replaces the momentum-smoothed gradient with its nearest semi-orthogonal matrix via a fixed 5-step Newton-Schulz iteration (no SVD); 1D params stay on AdamW. Matches AdamW final loss using ~52% of training FLOPs.",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\nmuon_params = []\nadamw_params = []\nfor name, p in model.named_parameters():\n    if not p.requires_grad:\n        continue\n    is_hidden_2d = p.ndim == 2 and 'embed' not in name and 'lm_head' not in name\n    if is_hidden_2d:\n        muon_params.append(p)\n    else:\n        adamw_params.append(p)\n\noptim_muon = torch.optim.Muon(muon_params, lr=0.02, weight_decay=0.1, momentum=0.95, nesterov=True, ns_steps=5, adjust_lr_fn='match_rms_adamw')\noptim_adamw = torch.optim.AdamW(adamw_params, lr=3e-4, weight_decay=0.01)\n\noptim_muon.zero_grad()\noptim_adamw.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim_muon.step()\noptim_adamw.step()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "torch.optim.Muon",
        "torch.optim.AdamW"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://docs.pytorch.org/docs/2.12/generated/torch.optim.Muon.html",

        "https://docs.pytorch.org/docs/2.12/optim.html",
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.16982"
      ],
      "usage": "Hybrid Muon+AdamW training optimizer for matmul-heavy candidates: the example code shows the parameter split, Muon settings, and AdamW companion optimizer.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "hybrid Muon+AdamW for matmul-heavy training with a tuned AdamW baseline",
        "split params by dimensionality/name: Muon for hidden 2D matrices, AdamW for embeddings, heads, bias, norm, and non-2D params",
        "tune Muon lr/momentum/ns_steps separately from AdamW lr/weight_decay"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no Muon on embedding/bias/norm/head/non-2D params and no comparison against undertuned AdamW",
        "no blind AdamW replacement in small CNN, tabular, inference, or memory-bound runs"
      ],

      "recommended": true,

      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "gram_newton_schulz_symmetric_gemm",
      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Gram Newton-Schulz symmetric/triangular fused GEMM: the Newton-Schulz step (used by Muon) is a symmetric product; a triangular fused kernel removes the wasted half of multiplies plus a DRAM round-trip cuBLAS incurs. Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",
      "api_symbols": [
        "custom_kernel:gram_newton_schulz",
        "custom_kernel:symmetric_gemm",
        "custom_kernel:triangular_fused_gemm"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://tridao.me/blog/2026/gram-newton-schulz/"
      ],
      "usage": "Kernel-level optimization for Muon/Newton-Schulz orthogonalization when profiler evidence shows the NS step is material. The example is a correctness fallback, not the fused production kernel.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "profile Muon optimizer-step time before considering the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel",
        "keep the reference Newton-Schulz implementation for correctness checks and fallback"
      ],

      "example_code": "import torch\n\nmuon_params = []\nadamw_params = []\nfor name, p in model.named_parameters():\n    if not p.requires_grad:\n        continue\n    is_hidden_2d = p.ndim == 2 and 'embed' not in name and 'lm_head' not in name\n    if is_hidden_2d:\n        muon_params.append(p)\n    else:\n        adamw_params.append(p)\n\noptim_muon = torch.optim.Muon(muon_params, lr=0.02, weight_decay=0.1, momentum=0.95, nesterov=True, ns_steps=5, adjust_lr_fn='match_rms_adamw')\noptim_adamw = torch.optim.AdamW(adamw_params, lr=3e-4, weight_decay=0.01)\n\noptim_muon.zero_grad()\noptim_adamw.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim_muon.step()\noptim_adamw.step()",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "gram_newton_schulz_symmetric_gemm",
      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Gram Newton-Schulz symmetric/triangular fused GEMM: the Newton-Schulz step (used by Muon) is a symmetric product; a triangular fused kernel removes the wasted half of multiplies plus a DRAM round-trip cuBLAS incurs. Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

      "example_code": "import os\nimport torch\nimport torch.distributed as dist\nimport torch.distributed._symmetric_memory as symm_mem\n\nrank = int(os.environ.get('LOCAL_RANK', '0'))\ndevice = torch.device('cuda', rank)\nopts = dist.ProcessGroupNCCL.Options()\nopts.config.cta_policy = dist.ProcessGroupNCCL.NCCL_CTA_POLICY_ZERO\ndist.init_process_group(backend='nccl', pg_options=opts, device_id=device)\nworld_size = dist.get_world_size()\n\nsymm_mem.set_backend('NCCL')\ngroup_name = dist.group.WORLD.group_name\ninp = symm_mem.empty(1024 * 1024, device=device)\nout = symm_mem.empty(1024 * 1024 * world_size, device=device)\nsymm_mem.rendezvous(inp, group=group_name)\nsymm_mem.rendezvous(out, group=group_name)\nwork = dist.all_gather_into_tensor(out, inp, async_op=True)\n# compute can be scheduled here on independent work\nwork.wait()",

      "feature_id": "fp8_rowwise_scaling",
      "category": "precision",
      "description": "FP8 row-wise (per-token) scaling: one fp8 scale factor per row of each operand instead of per-tensor; a single outlier row no longer forces a coarse scale that underflows the rest.       "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom torchao.float8 import Float8LinearConfig, Float8LinearRecipeName, convert_to_float8_training\n\nmodel = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(2048, 4096), nn.GELU(), nn.Linear(4096, 2048)).bfloat16().cuda()\nconfig = Float8LinearConfig.from_recipe_name(Float8LinearRecipeName.ROWWISE)\nconvert_to_float8_training(model, config=config)\nmodel = torch.compile(model)\nx = torch.randn(16, 2048, device='cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16)\ny = model(x)",

      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "description": "Gram Newton-Schulz symmetric/triangular fused GEMM: the Newton-Schulz step (used by Muon) is a symmetric product; a triangular fused kernel removes the wasted half of multiplies plus a DRAM round-trip cuBLAS incurs. Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom torchao.float8 import Float8LinearConfig, convert_to_float8_training\nfrom torchao.float8 import precompute_float8_dynamic_scale_for_fsdp\nfrom torch.distributed.fsdp import fully_shard\n\nmodel = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(4096, 4096), nn.GELU(), nn.Linear(4096, 4096)).bfloat16().cuda()\nconfig = Float8LinearConfig(enable_fsdp_float8_all_gather=True)\nconvert_to_float8_training(model, config=config)\nfor layer in model:\n    if isinstance(layer, nn.Linear):\n        fully_shard(layer)\nfully_shard(model)\n# In the distributed training loop, call after optimizer.step():\nprecompute_float8_dynamic_scale_for_fsdp(model)",
      "api_symbols": [
        "torchao.float8.Float8LinearConfig",
        "torchao.float8.Float8LinearConfig.enable_fsdp_float8_all_gather",
        "torchao.float8.precompute_float8_dynamic_scale_for_fsdp",
        "torchao.float8.convert_to_float8_training",
        "torch.distributed.fsdp.fully_shard"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/stable/api_reference/generated/torchao.float8.Float8LinearConfig.html"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,
      "limitations": "distributed FSDP2 training only; recommended=true means candidate when parameter all-gather communication is a measured bottleneck, not a single-GPU or default small-model setting; communication optimization uses FP8 all-gather while Tensor Cores are used by surrounding FP8 Linear compute; requires torchao float8 + FSDP2 stack and benchmark validation against BF16 communication; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true"
    }

      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "description": "Gram Newton-Schulz symmetric/triangular fused GEMM: the Newton-Schulz step (used by Muon) is a symmetric product; a triangular fused kernel removes the wasted half of multiplies plus a DRAM round-trip cuBLAS incurs. Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "import os\nimport torch\nimport torch.distributed as dist\nimport torch.distributed._symmetric_memory as symm_mem\n\nrank = int(os.environ.get('LOCAL_RANK', '0'))\ndevice = torch.device('cuda', rank)\nopts = dist.ProcessGroupNCCL.Options()\nopts.config.cta_policy = dist.ProcessGroupNCCL.NCCL_CTA_POLICY_ZERO\ndist.init_process_group(backend='nccl', pg_options=opts, device_id=device)\nworld_size = dist.get_world_size()\n\nsymm_mem.set_backend('NCCL')\ngroup_name = dist.group.WORLD.group_name\ninp = symm_mem.empty(1024 * 1024, device=device)\nout = symm_mem.empty(1024 * 1024 * world_size, device=device)\nsymm_mem.rendezvous(inp, group=group_name)\nsymm_mem.rendezvous(out, group=group_name)\nwork = dist.all_gather_into_tensor(out, inp, async_op=True)\n# compute can be scheduled here on independent work\nwork.wait()",

      "category": "optimizer",
      "description": "Muon optimizer: for 2D weight matrices, replaces the momentum-smoothed gradient with its nearest semi-orthogonal matrix via a fixed 5-step Newton-Schulz iteration (no SVD); 1D params stay on AdamW. Matches AdamW final loss using ~52% of training FLOPs.",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\nmuon_params = []\nadamw_params = []\nfor name, p in model.named_parameters():\n    if not p.requires_grad:\n        continue\n    is_hidden_2d = p.ndim == 2 and 'embed' not in name and 'lm_head' not in name\n    if is_hidden_2d:\n        muon_params.append(p)\n    else:\n        adamw_params.append(p)\n\noptim_muon = torch.optim.Muon(muon_params, lr=0.02, weight_decay=0.1, momentum=0.95, nesterov=True, ns_steps=5, adjust_lr_fn='match_rms_adamw')\noptim_adamw = torch.optim.AdamW(adamw_params, lr=3e-4, weight_decay=0.01)\n\noptim_muon.zero_grad()\noptim_adamw.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim_muon.step()\noptim_adamw.step()",

      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",
