      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom soap import SOAP\n\nmodel = nn.Linear(16, 4)\nx = torch.randn(8, 16)\nloss = model(x).pow(2).mean()\noptim = SOAP(model.parameters(), lr=3e-3, betas=(0.95, 0.95), weight_decay=0.01, precondition_frequency=10)\noptim.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim.step()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "soap.SOAP"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://github.com/nikhilvyas/SOAP",
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.11321"
      ],
      "usage": "Software optimizer feature for large-batch or optimizer-limited experiments after a tuned AdamW baseline; no hardware-specific SOAP pattern is defined in this file.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "try SOAP only after a tuned AdamW baseline in large-batch or optimizer-limited training",
        "track optimizer-step time, peak memory, and validation loss because preconditioner cost can dominate"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no SOAP as a universal default for small, latency-bound, or memory-constrained runs",
        "no adoption without verifying the third-party SOAP package/API in the runtime"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": false,
      "verified": false,

      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: classify SOAP as an experimental software optimizer candidate, not a hardware feature. Use when optimizer convergence is the bottleneck; skip when data/model execution dominates."
    },
    {
      "feature_id": "ademamix_optimizer",
      "category": "optimizer",
      "description": "AdEMAMix optimizer: augments Adam's fast gradient EMA with a second very-slow EMA (beta3 ~0.9999). A 1.3B model on 101B tokens matches AdamW on 197B (~51% token reduction to same loss). One extra EMA buffer.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom ademamix import AdEMAMix\n\nmodel = nn.Linear(16, 4)\nx = torch.randn(8, 16)\nloss = model(x).pow(2).mean()\nnum_iterations = 256000\noptim = AdEMAMix(params=model.parameters(), lr=1e-3, betas=(0.9, 0.999, 0.9999), alpha=8.0, beta3_warmup=num_iterations, alpha_warmup=num_iterations, weight_decay=0.1)\noptim.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim.step()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "ademamix.AdEMAMix"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://github.com/apple/ml-ademamix",
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.03137"
      ],
      "usage": "Software optimizer feature that augments Adam-style EMAs with a slow EMA; no hardware-specific AdEMAMix pattern is defined in this file.",
      "recommended_patterns": [

      ],

        "no comparison against untuned AdamW or mismatched token/step budgets"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": false,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "unconfirmed_software_optimizer_candidate",
      "limitations": "GeForce RTX 5070-specific benefit is not widely confirmed; treat AdEMAMix as a software optimizer experiment only for long-token or LLM-style training after a tuned AdamW baseline and an explicit optimizer-state memory budget.",
      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: recommend only as an optimizer experiment with memory budget and tuned baseline; it gives no inference speedup and no hardware-specific acceleration."
    },
    {
      "feature_id": "gram_newton_schulz_symmetric_gemm",
      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Gram Newton-Schulz symmetric/triangular fused GEMM: the Newton-Schulz step (used by Muon) is a symmetric product; a triangular fused kernel removes the wasted half of multiplies plus a DRAM round-trip cuBLAS incurs. Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",
      "api_symbols": [
        "custom_kernel:gram_newton_schulz",
        "custom_kernel:symmetric_gemm",
        "custom_kernel:triangular_fused_gemm"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://tridao.me/blog/2026/gram-newton-schulz/"
      ],
      "usage": "Kernel-level optimization for Muon/Newton-Schulz orthogonalization when profiler evidence shows the NS step is material. The example is a correctness fallback, not the fused production kernel.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "profile Muon optimizer-step time before considering the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel",
        "keep the reference Newton-Schulz implementation for correctness checks and fallback"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no use without Muon/Newton-Schulz workload",
        "no marking verified=true until benchmark and numerical parity are recorded"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "muon_newton_schulz_kernel_candidate",
      "limitations": "custom Gram/Newton-Schulz kernel optimization; recommended=true means candidate only when Muon/Newton-Schulz orthogonalization is material in the step profile, not a generic GEMM replacement; intended for Hopper/Blackwell-class tensor-core kernels; source is primary technical blog, not official vendor doc; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true",
      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: suggest this only after Muon has been selected and profiler evidence shows orthogonalization dominates; otherwise prefer normal optimizer tuning."
    }
  ]

      "feature_id": "gram_newton_schulz_symmetric_gemm",
      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Gram Newton-Schulz symmetric/triangular fused GEMM: the Newton-Schulz step (used by Muon) is a symmetric product; a triangular fused kernel removes the wasted half of multiplies plus a DRAM round-trip cuBLAS incurs. Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",
      "api_symbols": [
        "custom_kernel:gram_newton_schulz",

        "https://tridao.me/blog/2026/gram-newton-schulz/"
      ],

      "recommended_patterns": [
        "profile Muon optimizer-step time before considering the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel",

      "description": "Muon optimizer: for 2D weight matrices, replaces the momentum-smoothed gradient with its nearest semi-orthogonal matrix via a fixed 5-step Newton-Schulz iteration (no SVD); 1D params stay on AdamW. Matches AdamW final loss using ~52% of training FLOPs.",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\nmuon_params = []\nadamw_params = []\nfor name, p in model.named_parameters():\n    if not p.requires_grad:\n        continue\n    is_hidden_2d = p.ndim == 2 and 'embed' not in name and 'lm_head' not in name\n    if is_hidden_2d:\n        muon_params.append(p)\n    else:\n        adamw_params.append(p)\n\noptim_muon = torch.optim.Muon(muon_params, lr=0.02, weight_decay=0.1, momentum=0.95, nesterov=True, ns_steps=5, adjust_lr_fn='match_rms_adamw')\noptim_adamw = torch.optim.AdamW(adamw_params, lr=3e-4, weight_decay=0.01)\n\noptim_muon.zero_grad()\noptim_adamw.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim_muon.step()\noptim_adamw.step()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "torch.optim.Muon",
        "torch.optim.AdamW"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://docs.pytorch.org/docs/2.12/generated/torch.optim.Muon.html",
        "https://docs.pytorch.org/docs/2.12/generated/torch.optim.AdamW.html",
        "https://docs.pytorch.org/docs/2.12/optim.html",
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.16982"
      ],
      "usage": "Hybrid Muon+AdamW training optimizer for matmul-heavy candidates: the example code shows the parameter split, Muon settings, and AdamW companion optimizer.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "hybrid Muon+AdamW for matmul-heavy training with a tuned AdamW baseline",
        "split params by dimensionality/name: Muon for hidden 2D matrices, AdamW for embeddings, heads, bias, norm, and non-2D params",
        "tune Muon lr/momentum/ns_steps separately from AdamW lr/weight_decay"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no Muon on embedding/bias/norm/head/non-2D params and no comparison against undertuned AdamW",
        "no blind AdamW replacement in small CNN, tabular, inference, or memory-bound runs"
      ],
      "support_level": "supported",
      "recommended": false,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "optimizer_configurability_and_memory_budget_only",
      "limitations": "software training optimizer; requires a PyTorch build that exposes torch.optim.Muon; no hardware-specific acceleration is required; use only on selected 2D hidden weights and keep AdamW for embeddings/bias/norm/head/non-2D params; benchmark against tuned AdamW; not an inference feature",
      "notes": "no Muon-specific card tune; keep optimizer configurable and include optimizer state in memory planning"
    },
    {
      "feature_id": "soap_optimizer",
      "category": "optimizer",
      "description": "SOAP optimizer: Shampoo run in the eigenbasis of its own preconditioner, then plain Adam in that rotated space; amortizes eigendecomposition. >40% fewer iterations / >35% wall-clock vs AdamW to same loss in large-batch regime.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom soap import SOAP\n\nmodel = nn.Linear(16, 4)\nx = torch.randn(8, 16)\nloss = model(x).pow(2).mean()\noptim = SOAP(model.parameters(), lr=3e-3, betas=(0.95, 0.95), weight_decay=0.01, precondition_frequency=10)\noptim.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim.step()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "soap.SOAP"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://github.com/nikhilvyas/SOAP",
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.11321"
      ],
      "usage": "Software optimizer feature for large-batch or optimizer-limited experiments after a tuned AdamW baseline; no hardware-specific SOAP pattern is defined in this file.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "try SOAP only after a tuned AdamW baseline in large-batch or optimizer-limited training",
        "track optimizer-step time, peak memory, and validation loss because preconditioner cost can dominate"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no SOAP as a universal default for small, latency-bound, or memory-constrained runs",
        "no adoption without verifying the third-party SOAP package/API in the runtime"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": false,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "unconfirmed_software_optimizer_candidate",
      "limitations": "NVIDIA A100 PCIe 80GB-specific benefit is not widely confirmed; treat SOAP as a software optimizer experiment only after a tuned AdamW baseline, with optimizer-step time, peak memory, and validation loss measured before adoption.",
      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: classify SOAP as an experimental software optimizer candidate, not a hardware feature. Use when optimizer convergence is the bottleneck; skip when data/model execution dominates."
    },
    {
      "feature_id": "ademamix_optimizer",
      "category": "optimizer",
      "description": "AdEMAMix optimizer: augments Adam's fast gradient EMA with a second very-slow EMA (beta3 ~0.9999). A 1.3B model on 101B tokens matches AdamW on 197B (~51% token reduction to same loss). One extra EMA buffer.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom ademamix import AdEMAMix\n\nmodel = nn.Linear(16, 4)\nx = torch.randn(8, 16)\nloss = model(x).pow(2).mean()\nnum_iterations = 256000\noptim = AdEMAMix(params=model.parameters(), lr=1e-3, betas=(0.9, 0.999, 0.9999), alpha=8.0, beta3_warmup=num_iterations, alpha_warmup=num_iterations, weight_decay=0.1)\noptim.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim.step()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "ademamix.AdEMAMix"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://github.com/apple/ml-ademamix",
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.03137"
      ],
      "usage": "Software optimizer feature that augments Adam-style EMAs with a slow EMA; no hardware-specific AdEMAMix pattern is defined in this file.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "candidate for long-token or LLM-style training where AdamW token efficiency is the bottleneck",
        "budget one extra EMA buffer and tune alpha/beta3 warmups with the LR schedule"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no AdEMAMix as a drop-in default for short, small-data, or memory-constrained runs",
        "no comparison against untuned AdamW or mismatched token/step budgets"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": false,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "unconfirmed_software_optimizer_candidate",
      "limitations": "NVIDIA A100 PCIe 80GB-specific benefit is not widely confirmed; treat AdEMAMix as a software optimizer experiment only for long-token or LLM-style training after a tuned AdamW baseline and an explicit optimizer-state memory budget.",
      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: recommend only as an optimizer experiment with memory budget and tuned baseline; it gives no inference speedup and no hardware-specific acceleration."
    }
  ]

        "https://github.com/nikhilvyas/SOAP",
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.11321"
      ],
      "usage": "Software optimizer feature for large-batch or optimizer-limited experiments after a tuned AdamW baseline; no hardware-specific SOAP pattern is defined in this file.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "try SOAP only after a tuned AdamW baseline in large-batch or optimizer-limited training",
        "track optimizer-step time, peak memory, and validation loss because preconditioner cost can dominate"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no SOAP as a universal default for small, latency-bound, or memory-constrained runs",
        "no adoption without verifying the third-party SOAP package/API in the runtime"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": false,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "unconfirmed_software_optimizer_candidate",
      "limitations": "NVIDIA A16-specific benefit is not widely confirmed; treat SOAP as a software optimizer experiment only after a tuned AdamW baseline, with optimizer-step time, peak memory, and validation loss measured before adoption.",
      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: classify SOAP as an experimental software optimizer candidate, not a hardware feature. Use when optimizer convergence is the bottleneck; skip when data/model execution dominates."
    },
    {
      "feature_id": "ademamix_optimizer",
      "category": "optimizer",
      "description": "AdEMAMix optimizer: augments Adam's fast gradient EMA with a second very-slow EMA (beta3 ~0.9999). A 1.3B model on 101B tokens matches AdamW on 197B (~51% token reduction to same loss). One extra EMA buffer.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom ademamix import AdEMAMix\n\nmodel = nn.Linear(16, 4)\nx = torch.randn(8, 16)\nloss = model(x).pow(2).mean()\nnum_iterations = 256000\noptim = AdEMAMix(params=model.parameters(), lr=1e-3, betas=(0.9, 0.999, 0.9999), alpha=8.0, beta3_warmup=num_iterations, alpha_warmup=num_iterations, weight_decay=0.1)\noptim.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim.step()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "ademamix.AdEMAMix"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://github.com/apple/ml-ademamix",
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.03137"
      ],
      "usage": "Software optimizer feature that augments Adam-style EMAs with a slow EMA; no hardware-specific AdEMAMix pattern is defined in this file.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "candidate for long-token or LLM-style training where AdamW token efficiency is the bottleneck",
        "budget one extra EMA buffer and tune alpha/beta3 warmups with the LR schedule"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no AdEMAMix as a drop-in default for short, small-data, or memory-constrained runs",
        "no comparison against untuned AdamW or mismatched token/step budgets"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": false,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "unconfirmed_software_optimizer_candidate",
      "limitations": "NVIDIA A16-specific benefit is not widely confirmed; treat AdEMAMix as a software optimizer experiment only for long-token or LLM-style training after a tuned AdamW baseline and an explicit optimizer-state memory budget.",
      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: recommend only as an optimizer experiment with memory budget and tuned baseline; it gives no inference speedup and no hardware-specific acceleration."
    }
  ]

      "feature_id": "muon_optimizer",
      "category": "optimizer",
      "description": "Muon optimizer: for 2D weight matrices, replaces the momentum-smoothed gradient with its nearest semi-orthogonal matrix via a fixed 5-step Newton-Schulz iteration (no SVD); 1D params stay on AdamW. Matches AdamW final loss using ~52% of training FLOPs.",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\nmuon_params = []\nadamw_params = []\nfor name, p in model.named_parameters():\n    if not p.requires_grad:\n        continue\n    is_hidden_2d = p.ndim == 2 and 'embed' not in name and 'lm_head' not in name\n    if is_hidden_2d:\n        muon_params.append(p)\n    else:\n        adamw_params.append(p)\n\noptim_muon = torch.optim.Muon(muon_params, lr=0.02, weight_decay=0.1, momentum=0.95, nesterov=True, ns_steps=5, adjust_lr_fn='match_rms_adamw')\noptim_adamw = torch.optim.AdamW(adamw_params, lr=3e-4, weight_decay=0.01)\n\noptim_muon.zero_grad()\noptim_adamw.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim_muon.step()\noptim_adamw.step()",

      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom soap import SOAP\n\nmodel = nn.Linear(16, 4)\nx = torch.randn(8, 16)\nloss = model(x).pow(2).mean()\noptim = SOAP(model.parameters(), lr=3e-3, betas=(0.95, 0.95), weight_decay=0.01, precondition_frequency=10)\noptim.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim.step()",

      "description": "Muon optimizer: for 2D weight matrices, replaces the momentum-smoothed gradient with its nearest semi-orthogonal matrix via a fixed 5-step Newton-Schulz iteration (no SVD); 1D params stay on AdamW. Matches AdamW final loss using ~52% of training FLOPs.",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\nmuon_params = []\nadamw_params = []\nfor name, p in model.named_parameters():\n    if not p.requires_grad:\n        continue\n    is_hidden_2d = p.ndim == 2 and 'embed' not in name and 'lm_head' not in name\n    if is_hidden_2d:\n        muon_params.append(p)\n    else:\n        adamw_params.append(p)\n\noptim_muon = torch.optim.Muon(muon_params, lr=0.02, weight_decay=0.1, momentum=0.95, nesterov=True, ns_steps=5, adjust_lr_fn='match_rms_adamw')\noptim_adamw = torch.optim.AdamW(adamw_params, lr=3e-4, weight_decay=0.01)\n\noptim_muon.zero_grad()\noptim_adamw.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim_muon.step()\noptim_adamw.step()",

      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom soap import SOAP\n\nmodel = nn.Linear(16, 4)\nx = torch.randn(8, 16)\nloss = model(x).pow(2).mean()\noptim = SOAP(model.parameters(), lr=3e-3, betas=(0.95, 0.95), weight_decay=0.01, precondition_frequency=10)\noptim.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim.step()",

    {
      "feature_id": "gram_newton_schulz_symmetric_gemm",
      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Gram Newton-Schulz symmetric/triangular fused GEMM: the Newton-Schulz step (used by Muon) is a symmetric product; a triangular fused kernel removes the wasted half of multiplies plus a DRAM round-trip cuBLAS incurs. Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",
      "api_symbols": [
        "custom_kernel:gram_newton_schulz",
        "custom_kernel:symmetric_gemm",
        "custom_kernel:triangular_fused_gemm"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://tridao.me/blog/2026/gram-newton-schulz/"
      ],
      "usage": "Kernel-level optimization for Muon/Newton-Schulz orthogonalization when profiler evidence shows the NS step is material. The example is a correctness fallback, not the fused production kernel.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "profile Muon optimizer-step time before considering the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel",
        "keep the reference Newton-Schulz implementation for correctness checks and fallback"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no use without Muon/Newton-Schulz workload",
        "no marking verified=true until benchmark and numerical parity are recorded"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "muon_newton_schulz_kernel_candidate",
      "limitations": "custom Gram/Newton-Schulz kernel optimization; recommended=true means candidate only when Muon/Newton-Schulz orthogonalization is material in the step profile, not a generic GEMM replacement; intended for Hopper/Blackwell-class tensor-core kernels; source is primary technical blog, not official vendor doc; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true",

      "source_urls": [
        "https://github.com/apple/ml-ademamix",
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.03137"
      ],
      "usage": "Software optimizer feature that augments Adam-style EMAs with a slow EMA; no hardware-specific AdEMAMix pattern is defined in this file.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "candidate for long-token or LLM-style training where AdamW token efficiency is the bottleneck",
        "budget one extra EMA buffer and tune alpha/beta3 warmups with the LR schedule"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no AdEMAMix as a drop-in default for short, small-data, or memory-constrained runs",
        "no comparison against untuned AdamW or mismatched token/step budgets"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": false,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "unconfirmed_software_optimizer_candidate",
      "limitations": "NVIDIA GB200 Grace Blackwell Superchip-specific benefit is not widely confirmed; treat AdEMAMix as a software optimizer experiment only for long-token or LLM-style training after a tuned AdamW baseline and an explicit optimizer-state memory budget.",
      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: recommend only as an optimizer experiment with memory budget and tuned baseline; it gives no inference speedup and no hardware-specific acceleration."
    },
    {
      "feature_id": "gram_newton_schulz_symmetric_gemm",
      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Gram Newton-Schulz symmetric/triangular fused GEMM: the Newton-Schulz step (used by Muon) is a symmetric product; a triangular fused kernel removes the wasted half of multiplies plus a DRAM round-trip cuBLAS incurs. Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",
      "api_symbols": [
        "custom_kernel:gram_newton_schulz",
        "custom_kernel:symmetric_gemm",
        "custom_kernel:triangular_fused_gemm"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://tridao.me/blog/2026/gram-newton-schulz/"
      ],
      "usage": "Kernel-level optimization for Muon/Newton-Schulz orthogonalization when profiler evidence shows the NS step is material. The example is a correctness fallback, not the fused production kernel.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "profile Muon optimizer-step time before considering the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel",
        "keep the reference Newton-Schulz implementation for correctness checks and fallback"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no use without Muon/Newton-Schulz workload",
        "no marking verified=true until benchmark and numerical parity are recorded"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "muon_newton_schulz_kernel_candidate",
      "limitations": "custom Gram/Newton-Schulz kernel optimization; recommended=true means candidate only when Muon/Newton-Schulz orthogonalization is material in the step profile, not a generic GEMM replacement; intended for Hopper/Blackwell-class tensor-core kernels; source is primary technical blog, not official vendor doc; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true",
      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: suggest this only after Muon has been selected and profiler evidence shows orthogonalization dominates; otherwise prefer normal optimizer tuning."
    }
  ]
}

      "description": "Muon optimizer: for 2D weight matrices, replaces the momentum-smoothed gradient with its nearest semi-orthogonal matrix via a fixed 5-step Newton-Schulz iteration (no SVD); 1D params stay on AdamW. Matches AdamW final loss using ~52% of training FLOPs.",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\nmuon_params = []\nadamw_params = []\nfor name, p in model.named_parameters():\n    if not p.requires_grad:\n        continue\n    is_hidden_2d = p.ndim == 2 and 'embed' not in name and 'lm_head' not in name\n    if is_hidden_2d:\n        muon_params.append(p)\n    else:\n        adamw_params.append(p)\n\noptim_muon = torch.optim.Muon(muon_params, lr=0.02, weight_decay=0.1, momentum=0.95, nesterov=True, ns_steps=5, adjust_lr_fn='match_rms_adamw')\noptim_adamw = torch.optim.AdamW(adamw_params, lr=3e-4, weight_decay=0.01)\n\noptim_muon.zero_grad()\noptim_adamw.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim_muon.step()\noptim_adamw.step()",

      "feature_id": "gram_newton_schulz_symmetric_gemm",
      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Gram Newton-Schulz symmetric/triangular fused GEMM: the Newton-Schulz step (used by Muon) is a symmetric product; a triangular fused kernel removes the wasted half of multiplies plus a DRAM round-trip cuBLAS incurs. Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",
      "api_symbols": [

        "profile Muon optimizer-step time before considering the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel",

      "description": "Gram Newton-Schulz symmetric/triangular fused GEMM: the Newton-Schulz step (used by Muon) is a symmetric product; a triangular fused kernel removes the wasted half of multiplies plus a DRAM round-trip cuBLAS incurs. Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",
      "api_symbols": [
        "custom_kernel:gram_newton_schulz",
        "custom_kernel:symmetric_gemm",
        "custom_kernel:triangular_fused_gemm"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://tridao.me/blog/2026/gram-newton-schulz/"
      ],
      "usage": "Kernel-level optimization for Muon/Newton-Schulz orthogonalization when profiler evidence shows the NS step is material. The example is a correctness fallback, not the fused production kernel.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "profile Muon optimizer-step time before considering the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel",
        "keep the reference Newton-Schulz implementation for correctness checks and fallback"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no use without Muon/Newton-Schulz workload",
        "no marking verified=true until benchmark and numerical parity are recorded"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "muon_newton_schulz_kernel_candidate",
      "limitations": "custom Gram/Newton-Schulz kernel optimization; recommended=true means candidate only when Muon/Newton-Schulz orthogonalization is material in the step profile, not a generic GEMM replacement; intended for Hopper/Blackwell-class tensor-core kernels; source is primary technical blog, not official vendor doc; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true",
      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: suggest this only after Muon has been selected and profiler evidence shows orthogonalization dominates; otherwise prefer normal optimizer tuning."
    }
  ]
}

      "description": "SOAP optimizer: Shampoo run in the eigenbasis of its own preconditioner, then plain Adam in that rotated space; amortizes eigendecomposition. >40% fewer iterations / >35% wall-clock vs AdamW to same loss in large-batch regime.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom soap import SOAP\n\nmodel = nn.Linear(16, 4)\nx = torch.randn(8, 16)\nloss = model(x).pow(2).mean()\noptim = SOAP(model.parameters(), lr=3e-3, betas=(0.95, 0.95), weight_decay=0.01, precondition_frequency=10)\noptim.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim.step()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "soap.SOAP"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://github.com/nikhilvyas/SOAP",
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.11321"
      ],
      "usage": "Software optimizer feature for large-batch or optimizer-limited experiments after a tuned AdamW baseline; no hardware-specific SOAP pattern is defined in this file.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "try SOAP only after a tuned AdamW baseline in large-batch or optimizer-limited training",
        "track optimizer-step time, peak memory, and validation loss because preconditioner cost can dominate"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no SOAP as a universal default for small, latency-bound, or memory-constrained runs",
        "no adoption without verifying the third-party SOAP package/API in the runtime"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": false,
      "verified": false,

      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: classify SOAP as an experimental software optimizer candidate, not a hardware feature. Use when optimizer convergence is the bottleneck; skip when data/model execution dominates."
    },
    {
      "feature_id": "ademamix_optimizer",
      "category": "optimizer",
      "description": "AdEMAMix optimizer: augments Adam's fast gradient EMA with a second very-slow EMA (beta3 ~0.9999). A 1.3B model on 101B tokens matches AdamW on 197B (~51% token reduction to same loss). One extra EMA buffer.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom ademamix import AdEMAMix\n\nmodel = nn.Linear(16, 4)\nx = torch.randn(8, 16)\nloss = model(x).pow(2).mean()\nnum_iterations = 256000\noptim = AdEMAMix(params=model.parameters(), lr=1e-3, betas=(0.9, 0.999, 0.9999), alpha=8.0, beta3_warmup=num_iterations, alpha_warmup=num_iterations, weight_decay=0.1)\noptim.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim.step()",

        "budget one extra EMA buffer and tune alpha/beta3 warmups with the LR schedule"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no AdEMAMix as a drop-in default for short, small-data, or memory-constrained runs",
        "no comparison against untuned AdamW or mismatched token/step budgets"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": false,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "unconfirmed_software_optimizer_candidate",
      "limitations": "NVIDIA H200 SXM 141GB-specific benefit is not widely confirmed; treat AdEMAMix as a software optimizer experiment only for long-token or LLM-style training after a tuned AdamW baseline and an explicit optimizer-state memory budget.",
      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: recommend only as an optimizer experiment with memory budget and tuned baseline; it gives no inference speedup and no hardware-specific acceleration."
    },
    {
      "feature_id": "gram_newton_schulz_symmetric_gemm",
      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Gram Newton-Schulz symmetric/triangular fused GEMM: the Newton-Schulz step (used by Muon) is a symmetric product; a triangular fused kernel removes the wasted half of multiplies plus a DRAM round-trip cuBLAS incurs. Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",
      "api_symbols": [
        "custom_kernel:gram_newton_schulz",
        "custom_kernel:symmetric_gemm",
        "custom_kernel:triangular_fused_gemm"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://tridao.me/blog/2026/gram-newton-schulz/"
      ],
      "usage": "Kernel-level optimization for Muon/Newton-Schulz orthogonalization when profiler evidence shows the NS step is material. The example is a correctness fallback, not the fused production kernel.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "profile Muon optimizer-step time before considering the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel",
        "keep the reference Newton-Schulz implementation for correctness checks and fallback"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no use without Muon/Newton-Schulz workload",
        "no marking verified=true until benchmark and numerical parity are recorded"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "muon_newton_schulz_kernel_candidate",
      "limitations": "custom Gram/Newton-Schulz kernel optimization; recommended=true means candidate only when Muon/Newton-Schulz orthogonalization is material in the step profile, not a generic GEMM replacement; intended for Hopper/Blackwell-class tensor-core kernels; source is primary technical blog, not official vendor doc; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true",
      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: suggest this only after Muon has been selected and profiler evidence shows orthogonalization dominates; otherwise prefer normal optimizer tuning."
    }
  ]

        "no adoption without verifying the third-party SOAP package/API in the runtime"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": false,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "unconfirmed_software_optimizer_candidate",
      "limitations": "NVIDIA L40-specific benefit is not widely confirmed; treat SOAP as a software optimizer experiment only after a tuned AdamW baseline, with optimizer-step time, peak memory, and validation loss measured before adoption.",
      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: classify SOAP as an experimental software optimizer candidate, not a hardware feature. Use when optimizer convergence is the bottleneck; skip when data/model execution dominates."
    },
    {
      "feature_id": "ademamix_optimizer",
      "category": "optimizer",
      "description": "AdEMAMix optimizer: augments Adam's fast gradient EMA with a second very-slow EMA (beta3 ~0.9999). A 1.3B model on 101B tokens matches AdamW on 197B (~51% token reduction to same loss). One extra EMA buffer.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom ademamix import AdEMAMix\n\nmodel = nn.Linear(16, 4)\nx = torch.randn(8, 16)\nloss = model(x).pow(2).mean()\nnum_iterations = 256000\noptim = AdEMAMix(params=model.parameters(), lr=1e-3, betas=(0.9, 0.999, 0.9999), alpha=8.0, beta3_warmup=num_iterations, alpha_warmup=num_iterations, weight_decay=0.1)\noptim.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim.step()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "ademamix.AdEMAMix"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://github.com/apple/ml-ademamix",
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.03137"
      ],
      "usage": "Software optimizer feature that augments Adam-style EMAs with a slow EMA; no hardware-specific AdEMAMix pattern is defined in this file.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "candidate for long-token or LLM-style training where AdamW token efficiency is the bottleneck",
        "budget one extra EMA buffer and tune alpha/beta3 warmups with the LR schedule"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no AdEMAMix as a drop-in default for short, small-data, or memory-constrained runs",
        "no comparison against untuned AdamW or mismatched token/step budgets"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": false,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "unconfirmed_software_optimizer_candidate",
      "limitations": "NVIDIA L40-specific benefit is not widely confirmed; treat AdEMAMix as a software optimizer experiment only for long-token or LLM-style training after a tuned AdamW baseline and an explicit optimizer-state memory budget.",
      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: recommend only as an optimizer experiment with memory budget and tuned baseline; it gives no inference speedup and no hardware-specific acceleration."
    }

{
  "found": true,
  "node_id": "hw:nvidia.ada_lovelace.l40s.spec",

  "stage_filter": "optimizer",
  "feature_count": 3,
  "features": [
    {
      "feature_id": "muon_optimizer",
      "category": "optimizer",
      "description": "Muon optimizer: for 2D weight matrices, replaces the momentum-smoothed gradient with its nearest semi-orthogonal matrix via a fixed 5-step Newton-Schulz iteration (no SVD); 1D params stay on AdamW. Matches AdamW final loss using ~52% of training FLOPs.",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\nmuon_params = []\nadamw_params = []\nfor name, p in model.named_parameters():\n    if not p.requires_grad:\n        continue\n    is_hidden_2d = p.ndim == 2 and 'embed' not in name and 'lm_head' not in name\n    if is_hidden_2d:\n        muon_params.append(p)\n    else:\n        adamw_params.append(p)\n\noptim_muon = torch.optim.Muon(muon_params, lr=0.02, weight_decay=0.1, momentum=0.95, nesterov=True, ns_steps=5, adjust_lr_fn='match_rms_adamw')\noptim_adamw = torch.optim.AdamW(adamw_params, lr=3e-4, weight_decay=0.01)\n\noptim_muon.zero_grad()\noptim_adamw.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim_muon.step()\noptim_adamw.step()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "torch.optim.Muon",
        "torch.optim.AdamW"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://docs.pytorch.org/docs/2.12/generated/torch.optim.Muon.html",
        "https://docs.pytorch.org/docs/2.12/generated/torch.optim.AdamW.html",
        "https://docs.pytorch.org/docs/2.12/optim.html",
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.16982"
      ],

        "tune Muon lr/momentum/ns_steps separately from AdamW lr/weight_decay"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [

      "category": "optimizer",

      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom soap import SOAP\n\nmodel = nn.Linear(16, 4)\nx = torch.randn(8, 16)\nloss = model(x).pow(2).mean()\noptim = SOAP(model.parameters(), lr=3e-3, betas=(0.95, 0.95), weight_decay=0.01, precondition_frequency=10)\noptim.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim.step()",

      "category": "optimizer",
      "description": "Muon optimizer: for 2D weight matrices, replaces the momentum-smoothed gradient with its nearest semi-orthogonal matrix via a fixed 5-step Newton-Schulz iteration (no SVD); 1D params stay on AdamW. Matches AdamW final loss using ~52% of training FLOPs.",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\nmuon_params = []\nadamw_params = []\nfor name, p in model.named_parameters():\n    if not p.requires_grad:\n        continue\n    is_hidden_2d = p.ndim == 2 and 'embed' not in name and 'lm_head' not in name\n    if is_hidden_2d:\n        muon_params.append(p)\n    else:\n        adamw_params.append(p)\n\noptim_muon = torch.optim.Muon(muon_params, lr=0.02, weight_decay=0.1, momentum=0.95, nesterov=True, ns_steps=5, adjust_lr_fn='match_rms_adamw')\noptim_adamw = torch.optim.AdamW(adamw_params, lr=3e-4, weight_decay=0.01)\n\noptim_muon.zero_grad()\noptim_adamw.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim_muon.step()\noptim_adamw.step()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "torch.optim.Muon",
        "torch.optim.AdamW"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://docs.pytorch.org/docs/2.12/generated/torch.optim.Muon.html",
        "https://docs.pytorch.org/docs/2.12/generated/torch.optim.AdamW.html",
        "https://docs.pytorch.org/docs/2.12/optim.html",
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.16982"
      ],
      "usage": "Hybrid Muon+AdamW training optimizer for matmul-heavy candidates: the example code shows the parameter split, Muon settings, and AdamW companion optimizer.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "hybrid Muon+AdamW for matmul-heavy training with a tuned AdamW baseline",
        "split params by dimensionality/name: Muon for hidden 2D matrices, AdamW for embeddings, heads, bias, norm, and non-2D params",
        "tune Muon lr/momentum/ns_steps separately from AdamW lr/weight_decay"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no Muon on embedding/bias/norm/head/non-2D params and no comparison against undertuned AdamW",
        "no blind AdamW replacement in small CNN, tabular, inference, or memory-bound runs"
      ],
      "support_level": "supported",
      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "matmul_heavy_training",
      "limitations": "software training optimizer; requires a PyTorch build that exposes torch.optim.Muon; no hardware-specific acceleration is required; use only on selected 2D hidden weights and keep AdamW for embeddings/bias/norm/head/non-2D params; benchmark against tuned AdamW; not an inference feature",
      "notes": "fused AdamW optimizer as strong baseline; tune Muon momentum/ns_steps when loss is unstable or throughput drops; no single LR blindly shared by Muon and AdamW"
    },
    {
      "feature_id": "soap_optimizer",
      "category": "optimizer",
      "description": "SOAP optimizer: Shampoo run in the eigenbasis of its own preconditioner, then plain Adam in that rotated space; amortizes eigendecomposition. >40% fewer iterations / >35% wall-clock vs AdamW to same loss in large-batch regime.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom soap import SOAP\n\nmodel = nn.Linear(16, 4)\nx = torch.randn(8, 16)\nloss = model(x).pow(2).mean()\noptim = SOAP(model.parameters(), lr=3e-3, betas=(0.95, 0.95), weight_decay=0.01, precondition_frequency=10)\noptim.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim.step()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "soap.SOAP"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://github.com/nikhilvyas/SOAP",
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.11321"
      ],
      "usage": "Software optimizer feature for large-batch or optimizer-limited experiments after a tuned AdamW baseline; no hardware-specific SOAP pattern is defined in this file.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "try SOAP only after a tuned AdamW baseline in large-batch or optimizer-limited training",
        "track optimizer-step time, peak memory, and validation loss because preconditioner cost can dominate"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no SOAP as a universal default for small, latency-bound, or memory-constrained runs",
        "no adoption without verifying the third-party SOAP package/API in the runtime"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": false,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "unconfirmed_software_optimizer_candidate",
      "limitations": "NVIDIA RTX 5000 Ada Generation-specific benefit is not widely confirmed; treat SOAP as a software optimizer experiment only after a tuned AdamW baseline, with optimizer-step time, peak memory, and validation loss measured before adoption.",
      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: classify SOAP as an experimental software optimizer candidate, not a hardware feature. Use when optimizer convergence is the bottleneck; skip when data/model execution dominates."
    },
    {
      "feature_id": "ademamix_optimizer",
      "category": "optimizer",
      "description": "AdEMAMix optimizer: augments Adam's fast gradient EMA with a second very-slow EMA (beta3 ~0.9999). A 1.3B model on 101B tokens matches AdamW on 197B (~51% token reduction to same loss). One extra EMA buffer.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom ademamix import AdEMAMix\n\nmodel = nn.Linear(16, 4)\nx = torch.randn(8, 16)\nloss = model(x).pow(2).mean()\nnum_iterations = 256000\noptim = AdEMAMix(params=model.parameters(), lr=1e-3, betas=(0.9, 0.999, 0.9999), alpha=8.0, beta3_warmup=num_iterations, alpha_warmup=num_iterations, weight_decay=0.1)\noptim.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim.step()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "ademamix.AdEMAMix"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://github.com/apple/ml-ademamix",
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.03137"
      ],
      "usage": "Software optimizer feature that augments Adam-style EMAs with a slow EMA; no hardware-specific AdEMAMix pattern is defined in this file.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "candidate for long-token or LLM-style training where AdamW token efficiency is the bottleneck",
        "budget one extra EMA buffer and tune alpha/beta3 warmups with the LR schedule"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no AdEMAMix as a drop-in default for short, small-data, or memory-constrained runs",
        "no comparison against untuned AdamW or mismatched token/step budgets"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": false,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "unconfirmed_software_optimizer_candidate",
      "limitations": "NVIDIA RTX 5000 Ada Generation-specific benefit is not widely confirmed; treat AdEMAMix as a software optimizer experiment only for long-token or LLM-style training after a tuned AdamW baseline and an explicit optimizer-state memory budget.",
      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: recommend only as an optimizer experiment with memory budget and tuned baseline; it gives no inference speedup and no hardware-specific acceleration."
    }
  ]

        "https://github.com/nikhilvyas/SOAP",
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.11321"
      ],
      "usage": "Software optimizer feature for large-batch or optimizer-limited experiments after a tuned AdamW baseline; no hardware-specific SOAP pattern is defined in this file.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "try SOAP only after a tuned AdamW baseline in large-batch or optimizer-limited training",
        "track optimizer-step time, peak memory, and validation loss because preconditioner cost can dominate"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no SOAP as a universal default for small, latency-bound, or memory-constrained runs",
        "no adoption without verifying the third-party SOAP package/API in the runtime"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": false,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "unconfirmed_software_optimizer_candidate",
      "limitations": "NVIDIA RTX A2000-specific benefit is not widely confirmed; treat SOAP as a software optimizer experiment only after a tuned AdamW baseline, with optimizer-step time, peak memory, and validation loss measured before adoption.",
      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: classify SOAP as an experimental software optimizer candidate, not a hardware feature. Use when optimizer convergence is the bottleneck; skip when data/model execution dominates."
    },
    {
      "feature_id": "ademamix_optimizer",
      "category": "optimizer",
      "description": "AdEMAMix optimizer: augments Adam's fast gradient EMA with a second very-slow EMA (beta3 ~0.9999). A 1.3B model on 101B tokens matches AdamW on 197B (~51% token reduction to same loss). One extra EMA buffer.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom ademamix import AdEMAMix\n\nmodel = nn.Linear(16, 4)\nx = torch.randn(8, 16)\nloss = model(x).pow(2).mean()\nnum_iterations = 256000\noptim = AdEMAMix(params=model.parameters(), lr=1e-3, betas=(0.9, 0.999, 0.9999), alpha=8.0, beta3_warmup=num_iterations, alpha_warmup=num_iterations, weight_decay=0.1)\noptim.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim.step()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "ademamix.AdEMAMix"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://github.com/apple/ml-ademamix",
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.03137"
      ],
      "usage": "Software optimizer feature that augments Adam-style EMAs with a slow EMA; no hardware-specific AdEMAMix pattern is defined in this file.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "candidate for long-token or LLM-style training where AdamW token efficiency is the bottleneck",
        "budget one extra EMA buffer and tune alpha/beta3 warmups with the LR schedule"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no AdEMAMix as a drop-in default for short, small-data, or memory-constrained runs",
        "no comparison against untuned AdamW or mismatched token/step budgets"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": false,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "unconfirmed_software_optimizer_candidate",
      "limitations": "NVIDIA RTX A2000-specific benefit is not widely confirmed; treat AdEMAMix as a software optimizer experiment only for long-token or LLM-style training after a tuned AdamW baseline and an explicit optimizer-state memory budget.",
      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: recommend only as an optimizer experiment with memory budget and tuned baseline; it gives no inference speedup and no hardware-specific acceleration."
    }

    {
      "feature_id": "muon_optimizer",
      "category": "optimizer",
      "description": "Muon optimizer: for 2D weight matrices, replaces the momentum-smoothed gradient with its nearest semi-orthogonal matrix via a fixed 5-step Newton-Schulz iteration (no SVD); 1D params stay on AdamW. Matches AdamW final loss using ~52% of training FLOPs.",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\nmuon_params = []\nadamw_params = []\nfor name, p in model.named_parameters():\n    if not p.requires_grad:\n        continue\n    is_hidden_2d = p.ndim == 2 and 'embed' not in name and 'lm_head' not in name\n    if is_hidden_2d:\n        muon_params.append(p)\n    else:\n        adamw_params.append(p)\n\noptim_muon = torch.optim.Muon(muon_params, lr=0.02, weight_decay=0.1, momentum=0.95, nesterov=True, ns_steps=5, adjust_lr_fn='match_rms_adamw')\noptim_adamw = torch.optim.AdamW(adamw_params, lr=3e-4, weight_decay=0.01)\n\noptim_muon.zero_grad()\noptim_adamw.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim_muon.step()\noptim_adamw.step()",

        "torch.optim.Muon",

      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom soap import SOAP\n\nmodel = nn.Linear(16, 4)\nx = torch.randn(8, 16)\nloss = model(x).pow(2).mean()\noptim = SOAP(model.parameters(), lr=3e-3, betas=(0.95, 0.95), weight_decay=0.01, precondition_frequency=10)\noptim.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim.step()",

    {
      "feature_id": "muon_optimizer",
      "category": "optimizer",
      "description": "Muon optimizer: for 2D weight matrices, replaces the momentum-smoothed gradient with its nearest semi-orthogonal matrix via a fixed 5-step Newton-Schulz iteration (no SVD); 1D params stay on AdamW. Matches AdamW final loss using ~52% of training FLOPs.",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\nmuon_params = []\nadamw_params = []\nfor name, p in model.named_parameters():\n    if not p.requires_grad:\n        continue\n    is_hidden_2d = p.ndim == 2 and 'embed' not in name and 'lm_head' not in name\n    if is_hidden_2d:\n        muon_params.append(p)\n    else:\n        adamw_params.append(p)\n\noptim_muon = torch.optim.Muon(muon_params, lr=0.02, weight_decay=0.1, momentum=0.95, nesterov=True, ns_steps=5, adjust_lr_fn='match_rms_adamw')\noptim_adamw = torch.optim.AdamW(adamw_params, lr=3e-4, weight_decay=0.01)\n\noptim_muon.zero_grad()\noptim_adamw.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim_muon.step()\noptim_adamw.step()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "torch.optim.Muon",
        "torch.optim.AdamW"
      ],
      "source_urls": [

        "tune Muon lr/momentum/ns_steps separately from AdamW lr/weight_decay"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no Muon on embedding/bias/norm/head/non-2D params and no comparison against undertuned AdamW",

      "notes": "Use Muon on selected 2D hidden weights and AdamW on embedding/bias/norm/head/non-2D params; tune Muon and AdamW learning rates separately; tune weight_decay per optimizer group; prefer adjust_lr_fn='match_rms_adamw' when reusing an AdamW LR schedule; compare against a tuned AdamW baseline."
    },
    {
      "feature_id": "soap_optimizer",
      "category": "optimizer",
      "description": "SOAP optimizer: Shampoo run in the eigenbasis of its own preconditioner, then plain Adam in that rotated space; amortizes eigendecomposition. >40% fewer iterations / >35% wall-clock vs AdamW to same loss in large-batch regime.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom soap import SOAP\n\nmodel = nn.Linear(16, 4)\nx = torch.randn(8, 16)\nloss = model(x).pow(2).mean()\noptim = SOAP(model.parameters(), lr=3e-3, betas=(0.95, 0.95), weight_decay=0.01, precondition_frequency=10)\noptim.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim.step()",

      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: classify SOAP as an experimental software optimizer candidate, not a hardware feature. Use when optimizer convergence is the bottleneck; skip when data/model execution dominates."
    },
    {
      "feature_id": "ademamix_optimizer",
      "category": "optimizer",
      "description": "AdEMAMix optimizer: augments Adam's fast gradient EMA with a second very-slow EMA (beta3 ~0.9999). A 1.3B model on 101B tokens matches AdamW on 197B (~51% token reduction to same loss). One extra EMA buffer.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom ademamix import AdEMAMix\n\nmodel = nn.Linear(16, 4)\nx = torch.randn(8, 16)\nloss = model(x).pow(2).mean()\nnum_iterations = 256000\noptim = AdEMAMix(params=model.parameters(), lr=1e-3, betas=(0.9, 0.999, 0.9999), alpha=8.0, beta3_warmup=num_iterations, alpha_warmup=num_iterations, weight_decay=0.1)\noptim.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim.step()",

        "candidate for long-token or LLM-style training where AdamW token efficiency is the bottleneck",
        "budget one extra EMA buffer and tune alpha/beta3 warmups with the LR schedule"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no AdEMAMix as a drop-in default for short, small-data, or memory-constrained runs",

      "recommended": false,

      "feature_id": "gram_newton_schulz_symmetric_gemm",
      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Gram Newton-Schulz symmetric/triangular fused GEMM: the Newton-Schulz step (used by Muon) is a symmetric product; a triangular fused kernel removes the wasted half of multiplies plus a DRAM round-trip cuBLAS incurs. Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch\n\n# Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7):\n    X = X / (X.norm() + eps)\n    wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1]\n    for _ in range(ns_steps):\n        if wide:\n            G = X @ X.T\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X)\n        else:\n            G = X.T @ X\n            X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G)\n    return X\n\nmomentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,
      "limitations": "software technique for cross-entropy over a linear classifier/logit projection; strongest fit is large-vocabulary LM heads, but Apple documents the general logits = X @ A.T + b form; requires compatible PyTorch/Triton/CUDA stack for the Triton CCE path; little benefit when logits materialization is not a memory/time bottleneck; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true"
    },
    {
      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",
      "source_urls": [
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.13226"
      ],
      "support_level": "supported",
      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,
      "limitations": "data-ordering technique for text sequence training; no hardware-specific acceleration and no direct inference benefit; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true"
    },
    {
      "feature_id": "nvimagecodec_gpu_decode",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "nvImageCodec GPU image decode: decodes encoded image bytes (nvJPEG/nvJPEG2000/nvTIFF plus hardware JPEG decode on documented nvJPEG hardware [https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/nvjpeg/index.html]) directly into GPU memory, DLPack zero-copy to torch, decode of batch N+1 overlapping compute of batch N. Removes CPU-decode bottleneck for CV/imaging. ~2.18x faster JPEG loading. No-op for text/LLM training.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom nvidia import nvimgcodec\n\ndecoder = nvimgcodec.Decoder()\nnv_img = decoder.read('sample.jp2')\ncap = nv_img.to_dlpack()\ntorch_img = torch.from_dlpack(cap)\nassert torch_img.is_cuda\nbatch = torch_img.permute(2, 0, 1).unsqueeze(0).contiguous()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "nvidia.nvimgcodec.Decoder",
        "nvidia.nvimgcodec.Encoder",
        "nvidia.nvimgcodec.Image.to_dlpack",
        "nvidia.nvimgcodec.as_image",
        "torch.from_dlpack",
        "torch.to_dlpack"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/nvimagecodec/samples/torch.html"
      ],
      "support_level": "supported",
      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,
      "limitations": "GPU image-codec data pipeline; recommended=true only for CV/imaging pipelines where CPU image decode is a measured bottleneck, not for text/LLM-only workloads or storage/augmentation-bound pipelines; NVIDIA nvJPEG hardware-acceleration docs only list Ampere A100/A30 within Ampere, so dedicated hardware JPEG decode is not assumed for this card; uses hybrid/software/GPU codec paths where available; codec/plugin support is format-dependent; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true"

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",
      "source_urls": [
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.13226"
      ],
      "support_level": "supported",
      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,
      "limitations": "data-ordering technique for text sequence training; no hardware-specific acceleration and no direct inference benefit; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true"
    },
    {
      "feature_id": "nvimagecodec_gpu_decode",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "nvImageCodec GPU image decode: decodes encoded image bytes (nvJPEG/nvJPEG2000/nvTIFF plus hardware JPEG decode on documented nvJPEG hardware [https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/nvjpeg/index.html]) directly into GPU memory, DLPack zero-copy to torch, decode of batch N+1 overlapping compute of batch N. Removes CPU-decode bottleneck for CV/imaging. ~2.18x faster JPEG loading. No-op for text/LLM training.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom nvidia import nvimgcodec\n\ndecoder = nvimgcodec.Decoder()\nnv_img = decoder.read('sample.jp2')\ncap = nv_img.to_dlpack()\ntorch_img = torch.from_dlpack(cap)\nassert torch_img.is_cuda\nbatch = torch_img.permute(2, 0, 1).unsqueeze(0).contiguous()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "nvidia.nvimgcodec.Decoder",
        "nvidia.nvimgcodec.Encoder",
        "nvidia.nvimgcodec.Image.to_dlpack",
        "nvidia.nvimgcodec.as_image",
        "torch.from_dlpack",
        "torch.to_dlpack"
      ],
      "source_urls": [
        "https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/nvimagecodec/samples/torch.html"

    },
    {
      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",
      "source_urls": [
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.13226"
      ],
      "support_level": "supported",
      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,

    },
    {
      "feature_id": "nvimagecodec_gpu_decode",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "nvImageCodec GPU image decode: decodes encoded image bytes (nvJPEG/nvJPEG2000/nvTIFF plus hardware JPEG decode on documented nvJPEG hardware [https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/nvjpeg/index.html]) directly into GPU memory, DLPack zero-copy to torch, decode of batch N+1 overlapping compute of batch N. Removes CPU-decode bottleneck for CV/imaging. ~2.18x faster JPEG loading. No-op for text/LLM training.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom nvidia import nvimgcodec\n\ndecoder = nvimgcodec.Decoder()\nnv_img = decoder.read('sample.jp2')\ncap = nv_img.to_dlpack()\ntorch_img = torch.from_dlpack(cap)\nassert torch_img.is_cuda\nbatch = torch_img.permute(2, 0, 1).unsqueeze(0).contiguous()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "nvidia.nvimgcodec.Decoder",
        "nvidia.nvimgcodec.Encoder",
        "nvidia.nvimgcodec.Image.to_dlpack",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom torchao.float8 import Float8LinearConfig, convert_to_float8_training\nfrom torchao.float8 import precompute_float8_dynamic_scale_for_fsdp\nfrom torch.distributed.fsdp import fully_shard\n\nmodel = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(4096, 4096), nn.GELU(), nn.Linear(4096, 4096)).bfloat16().cuda()\nconfig = Float8LinearConfig(enable_fsdp_float8_all_gather=True)\nconvert_to_float8_training(model, config=config)\nfor layer in model:\n    if isinstance(layer, nn.Linear):\n        fully_shard(layer)\nfully_shard(model)\n# In the distributed training loop, call after optimizer.step():\nprecompute_float8_dynamic_scale_for_fsdp(model)",
      "api_symbols": [
        "torchao.float8.Float8LinearConfig",
        "torchao.float8.Float8LinearConfig.enable_fsdp_float8_all_gather",
        "torchao.float8.precompute_float8_dynamic_scale_for_fsdp",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",
      "source_urls": [
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.13226"
      ],

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",
      "source_urls": [
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.13226"
      ],

      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom torchao.float8 import Float8LinearConfig, convert_to_float8_training\nfrom torchao.float8 import precompute_float8_dynamic_scale_for_fsdp\nfrom torch.distributed.fsdp import fully_shard\n\nmodel = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(4096, 4096), nn.GELU(), nn.Linear(4096, 4096)).bfloat16().cuda()\nconfig = Float8LinearConfig(enable_fsdp_float8_all_gather=True)\nconvert_to_float8_training(model, config=config)\nfor layer in model:\n    if isinstance(layer, nn.Linear):\n        fully_shard(layer)\nfully_shard(model)\n# In the distributed training loop, call after optimizer.step():\nprecompute_float8_dynamic_scale_for_fsdp(model)",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",
      "source_urls": [
        "https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.13226"
      ],

      "recommended": true,

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",
      "source_urls": [

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "fp8_all_gather_fsdp2",
      "category": "parallelism",
      "description": "FP8 all-gather in FSDP2: shards and all-gathers parameters in fp8 instead of bf16, halving the sharded-parameter communication volume in distributed training. Multi-GPU only. Built on torchao float8 + FSDP2.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom torch import nn\nfrom torchao.float8 import Float8LinearConfig, convert_to_float8_training\nfrom torchao.float8 import precompute_float8_dynamic_scale_for_fsdp\nfrom torch.distributed.fsdp import fully_shard\n\nmodel = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(4096, 4096), nn.GELU(), nn.Linear(4096, 4096)).bfloat16().cuda()\nconfig = Float8LinearConfig(enable_fsdp_float8_all_gather=True)\nconvert_to_float8_training(model, config=config)\nfor layer in model:\n    if isinstance(layer, nn.Linear):\n        fully_shard(layer)\nfully_shard(model)\n# In the distributed training loop, call after optimizer.step():\nprecompute_float8_dynamic_scale_for_fsdp(model)",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)):\n    buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths}\n    for doc in tokenized_docs:\n        n = len(doc)\n        for L in bucket_lengths:\n            for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L):\n                # never concatenate across documents\n                buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L])\n    return buckets\n\ndef sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps):\n    lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs])\n    max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1)))\n    active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1]\n    return active_lengths[-1]",
